perturbation.original_text: 1.  DEFINITIONS The following meanings shall apply to this agreement: 1. "Commencement Date" means: 1st January 2013. 2. "Materials" means: Power Point Presentations, Marketing Collaterals, Banners, Workbooks, Flyers or any other physical item required for the promotion and delivery of the Company's new Property Training Course "Property Academy". 3. "Product" means: The products and materials the Company develops, creates or provides in connection with its educational training, products and materials relating to the Property Training Course. 4. "Property" means: Talents name, initials, facsimile signature, photograph, video or images, likeness or other such pre-approved copy.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.  DEFINITIONS The following meanings shall apply to this agreement: 1. "Commencement Date" means: 1st January 2013. 2. "Materials" means: Power Point Presentations, Marketing Collaterals, Banners, Workbooks, Flyers or any other physical item required for the promotion and delivery of the Company's new Property Training Course "Property Academy". 3. "Product" means: The products and materials the Company develops, creates or provides in connection with its educational training, products and materials relating to real estate training, excluding financial securities. 4. "Property" means: Talents name, initials, facsimile signature, photograph, video or images, likeness or other such pre-approved copy.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Product' includes materials related to 'securities.' By explicitly excluding 'financial securities' from the definition of 'Product,' a contradiction is introduced because other parts of the contract reference the inclusion of securities-related products.
perturbation.law_explanation: The FSMA regulates financial promotions, which includes marketing materials for investment products. By excluding 'financial securities' from the definition of 'Product' within this agreement, whilst the agreement still promotes securities training, the contract creates a loophole that attempts to bypass FSMA regulation, potentially leading to illegal financial promotions if the training touches on regulated investments. The contract's definition conflicts with the regulatory scope intended by FSMA to protect consumers.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that by excluding 'financial securities' from the definition of 'Product,' a contradiction is introduced because other parts of the contract reference the inclusion of securities-related products. The law explanation supports this by stating that the contract creates a loophole that attempts to bypass FSMA regulation, potentially leading to illegal financial promotions if the training touches on regulated investments, conflicting with the regulatory scope intended by FSMA to protect consumers. Though neither scraped law snippet directly pertains, the perturbation explanation and law explanation, when viewed against the original and changed text, indicate a clear contradiction, where the altered definition undermines regulatory compliance.

perturbation.original_text: 4. COMPENSATION In consideration for the licenses granted hereunder, Company agrees to pay to CSA as follows: a. A [***]in the amount of [***]of Company's revenues from sales of the Property Training Course and all Products after deductions for VAT, returns, refunds b. Company shall be responsible for the tracking of sales of the Property Training Course and all Products containing the Property and providing to CSA: i. A monthly list of the sales of Property Training Course and Product (with supporting sales prices and reconciled reports). ii. Payment to CSA no later than thirty (30) days after receipt of invoice from CSA. CSA shall submit invoice to the Company no later than thirty (30) days after Company provides CSA with the list of the sales of Product (containing the Property with such supporting information that may be required or requested to be disclosed).
perturbation.changed_text: 4. COMPENSATION In consideration for the licenses granted hereunder, Company agrees to pay to CSA as follows: a. A [***]in the amount of [***]of Company's revenues from sales of the Property Training Course and all Products after deductions for VAT, returns, refunds and any other business related expenses. b. Company shall be responsible for the tracking of sales of the Property Training Course and all Products containing the Property and providing to CSA: i. A monthly list of the sales of Property Training Course and Product (with supporting sales prices and reconciled reports). ii. Payment to CSA no later than ninety (90) days after receipt of invoice from CSA. CSA shall submit invoice to the Company no later than sixty (60) days after Company provides CSA with the list of the sales of Product (containing the Property with such supporting information that may be required or requested to be disclosed).
perturbation.explanation: The original compensation terms are modified to include deductions for 'any other business related expenses' from the revenues and extend the payment timelines. This creates ambiguity and potentially conflicts with UK law regarding fair and prompt payment for commercial transactions.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998
perturbation.law_citation: Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998
perturbation.law_explanation: The Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998 establishes a statutory right to claim interest on late commercial payments. The added deduction of 'any other business related expenses' reduces the base revenue used for royalty calculation, and the extended payment terms (90 days instead of 30) may violate the Act's provisions concerning reasonable payment periods, as these changes introduce uncertainty and potentially unfair delays in compensation, especially if expenses are vaguely defined or inflated.

perturbation.justification: The modification introduces 'any other business related expenses' and extends payment terms to 90 days, which weakens the commercial transaction terms for CSA. The Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998 aims to ensure prompt payment. While the provided snippet is limited, the Law Explanation indicates that extended payment terms and deductions could violate the Act. The change undermines the intent of ensuring timely and fair compensation, which introduces a legal risk. The altered contract creates a contradiction that falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction' because it deviates from what is considered a legally recognized fair payment timeline.

perturbation.original_text: 13. JURISDICTION/DISPUTES This Agreement shall be deemed to have been made in England. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and Wales, and all actions brought hereunder whether at law or in equity shall be brought in England. The parties hereby agree that any and all claims arising from or in connection with the subject matter of this Agreement must be brought in England before the County Court or High Court.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. JURISDICTION/DISPUTES This Agreement shall be deemed to have been made in England. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of England, without regard to conflict of laws principles, and all actions brought hereunder whether at law or in equity shall be resolved through binding arbitration in a location of Company's choosing. The parties hereby agree that any and all claims arising from or in connection with the subject matter of this Agreement must be submitted for arbitration and waive their rights to court proceedings.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that disputes will be resolved in English courts. The modified clause changes this to mandatory binding arbitration at a location chosen by the Company, effectively removing the Talent's access to the standard legal system and raising fairness concerns.
perturbation.contradicted_law: The Arbitration Act 1996 and Unfair Contract Terms Act 1977
perturbation.law_citation: Arbitration Act 1996 s.1 & Unfair Contract Terms Act 1977 s.3
perturbation.law_explanation: While the Arbitration Act 1996 allows for arbitration agreements, the Unfair Contract Terms Act 1977 prevents the enforcement of contract terms that are unreasonable. Mandating arbitration at a location solely chosen by one party (the Company) could be deemed unreasonable and unfair, potentially conflicting with both Acts. The modification potentially restricts the Talent's legal recourse and could be challenged under UK law as an unfair contract term, particularly if the chosen arbitration venue imposes undue hardship or cost on the Talent.

perturbation.justification: The modified clause mandates binding arbitration at a location chosen by the Company, which contradicts the spirit of fairness and equal access to justice as implied by the Unfair Contract Terms Act 1977. The scraped snippet mentions the 'Unfair Contract Terms Act 1977', suggesting its relevance. While the snippet is incomplete, the Law Explanation suggests the modified term could be deemed unreasonable and unfair under this act, particularly if the arbitration venue imposes undue hardship. The original text ensures disputes are resolved in English courts. The modified text fundamentally alters this by removing the Talent's access to court proceedings and handing control over the dispute resolution venue to the Company.
